stage: Phase-2 co-carry base-difficulty probe (the cheap 'is there a hard regime?' check)
purpose: Eval-only, no-training probe: does a HARDER co-carry regime exist where the competent hand-built MATCHED pair (cooperative-impedance ego + matched impedance partner) itself stops trivially solving the task, AND degrades for coordination reasons (graceful coordination-strain) rather than numerical blow-up? Decides between (Option 2) building the full heterogeneity test in a genuinely-hard regime and (Option 3) concluding heterogeneity is not a cooperation-breaker on co-carry-class tasks. Board-endorsed base-failure criterion (Convening R-2026-06-C). Phase-2, NON-gating; does not touch Phase-1 or the M10 gate.

governance.matched_pair_only: cooperative-impedance ego + matched impedance partner (the 'base' config of the Rung-5 base_robustness_control); the ONLY thing varied is task difficulty.
governance.success_predicate_unchanged: The frozen joint predicate (placed AND level AND unstressed AND static AND settled) is NEVER weakened. Secondary sweeps only TIGHTEN it; the artifact-vs-real test classifies failures.
governance.immutability: Prior artifacts under spikes/results/** are immutable (I8); no schema bump of chamber.comm.SCHEMA_VERSION (I9) — this is a new JSON artifact type.
governance.reuses: Existing harness (chamber.benchmarks.cocarry_ph.build_cooperative_ego / build_partner_seat / rollout_pair), the embodiment-invariant coupling-stress instrument (chamber.envs.cocarry stress_measure='coupling'), and the existing difficulty knobs (drive_stiffness / goal radius / tilt limit). No new task class is built.

provenance.author: fsafaei
adr: ADR-026 §Open-questions ('Coupling stiffness is a task parameter, not a fixed constant')
ADR-026 §Validation-criteria (re-operationalization, not an axis cut, is the corrective)
provenance.fmax_grounding: spikes/results/cocarry/rung4c/cocarry_rung4c_eh_measurement.json (fmax_coupling_n = 365.608 = 1.25 x matched-Panda-pair coupling success-stress p99; matched pair rate at f_max = 1.0).

fixed_base_config.note: On main, make_cocarry_training_env has no stress_max param (a Rung-5 addition), so the env-internal success flag gates at the wrist ceiling 130 N and is IGNORED. Success is computed POST-HOC for every setting via the pure chamber.envs.cocarry.evaluate_cocarry_success(...) on the recorded raw metrics (centroid, max_tilt, max_stress=coupling force, both_static) at the f_max above. The matched hand-built controllers ignore reward, so rollout physics are byte-identical regardless of the (unused) stress_max/penalty params.

seed_disjointness: Disjoint from ALL prior co-carry seed sets (Rung-2 S 10000- / V 20000-, Rung-3 measurement 30000- / calibration 40000-, Rung-4 EH 50000- / 60000-, Rung-5 measurement 54000-).

primary_stiffness.knob: drive_stiffness (N/m) of the compliant freed-axis spring coupling; swept up from the K=8000 base toward rigid behaviour (K->inf approaches the rigid hard-locked weld). Damping scales at the rig's fixed 0.1 ratio.
primary_stiffness.method: Real rollouts of the matched pair at each K (12 seeds), recording per-step instantaneous trajectories (tilt_deg, coupling stress, centroid) for the artifact-vs-real test, plus per-episode metrics.
primary_stiffness.note: The Rung-0..4 RIGID weld is drive_stiffness=None (a hard axis-lock, a different mechanism). This sweep stays in the compliant-spring regime and cranks K; it is NOT the rigid hard-lock.

secondary_goal_radius.knob: goal_thresh (m) tightened in the success predicate (post-hoc re-evaluation on the K=8000 base rollouts; physics unchanged => cannot be numerically unstable).

secondary_tilt.knob: tilt_max (deg) tightened in the success predicate (post-hoc re-evaluation on the K=8000 base rollouts).

force_limit_variantB.status: NOT RUN in this probe (optional per the probe scope; keep it light). Recorded so the omission is explicit, not a silent cap.

artifact_vs_real_classification.rule_numerically_unstable_artifact: A setting is NUMERICAL_ARTIFACT (over-coupling / blow-up, the Rung-4 wall) iff ANY of: (a) any non-finite (NaN/Inf) tilt or coupling stress in any episode; (b) median-over-seeds of episode-max coupling stress >= stress_artifact_n (3 x f_max = 1096.8 N -- cf the learned-incumbent fight 854-1281 N); (c) median-over-seeds of episode-max tilt >= tilt_artifact_deg (60 deg -- the bar pendulums/drops, cf single-arm 72-89 deg). Such a setting is a numerical blow-up, NEVER a 'hard regime'.

success_bands_for_stable_settings.HARD_BUT_FEASIBLE: 0.10 <= success_rate <= 0.90 AND physics stable -- meaningfully below ~1.0 via graceful coordination-strain. THE TARGET.
success_bands_for_stable_settings.STABLE_INFEASIBLE: success_rate < 0.10 AND physics stable (bar held, predicate unmet) -- a stable hard failure, distinct from numerical blow-up; reported, but NOT a hard-but-feasible band.

decision_rule.HARD_BUT_FEASIBLE_BAND_EXISTS: iff >= 1 setting (primary stiffness OR secondary radius/tilt) classifies HARD_BUT_FEASIBLE (graceful, success in [0.10, 0.90], physics stable). => A genuinely-hard regime exists; testing heterogeneity there is meaningful. Next step = the full Option-2 heterogeneity test in that regime, properly instrumented (diversity-trained / partner-robust incumbent + the R-2026-06-C conditions). Report the setting(s) that define the band.
decision_rule.NO_FEASIBLE_HARD_REGIME_VIA_THESE_KNOBS: iff NO setting is HARD_BUT_FEASIBLE: the matched base stays >= 0.95 on every stable setting, and stiffness only ever transitions trivial -> NUMERICAL_ARTIFACT (no graceful band). => No buildable co-carry regime makes coordination strain a competent controller via these knobs -> supports concluding + reframing (a contact-rich DIFFERENT task class is a separate, bigger, gated decision -- NOT more stiffness).
decision_rule.report_distinction: The synthesis MUST distinguish WHICH knob defines any band: a coupling-stiffness (physics-gets-hard) band vs a predicate-tightening (precision-tolerance) band have different implications for a downstream heterogeneity test. Both satisfy the rule, but the distinction is reported honestly.
decision_rule.guard: If raising stiffness only ever yields NUMERICAL_ARTIFACT (never a graceful band), that IS the 'no feasible hard regime via stiffness' result -- report it; do not force it. Never call a numerical blow-up a 'hard regime'.
